entry.title: DRC - alert when invoice retrieval job fails
entry.content:
Hello,

we use DRC for E-Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 (Poland).

We have a hourly job that retrieves incoming invoices. There's been occurrences where the job has failed and didn't retrieve a few invoices. When this happens, one or more entries appear in the "Resolve errors" section of the DRC: from there I can reprocess the errors.

What I would like to know is if there's a way to receive an alert when this happens, or if I need to manually check the "Resolve errors".

Thanks, kind regards.

entry.title: Two Replacement Orders Created for One CBAR Returns Order
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am testing the Customer Returns (CBAR) process in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Hands-On Practice system.

I created a standard returns order using the following data:

  • Returns Order Reason: Standard Return (009)
  • Product: TG11
  • Returned Quantity: 4
  • Return Reason: Product Damaged (003)
  • Follow-Up Activity: Receive into Plant (0001)
  • Refund Type: Replacement Product
  • Refund Control: Create Replacement Order (P)

After processing the return, the system created two replacement orders instead of one.

I expected the system to create one replacement order for the returned quantity of 4.                                                                                           Thank you in advance for your support

entry.title: Re: BTP + Joule integration
entry.content: Thanks for the perspective. However, my requirement is specifically about Joule acting as an event‑driven orchestrator in a utility context — not just reactive to human triggers. In meter‑to‑cash and outage workflows, Joule needs to consume events directly from Event Mesh, apply AI reasoning, and then trigger SAP Build Process Automation workflows. The answer doesn’t fully address how Joule integrates with Event Mesh, CAP, and Integration Suite to achieve this, nor how it operationalizes workflows in S/4HANA utility processes. Without that, the architecture remains incomplete for real‑world utility scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Event‑driven architecture
entry.content:
Thanks for the input. My requirement is specifically about large‑scale utility workflows where Event Mesh must do more than just route events — it needs schema governance, replay, and filtering to support AI services at scale.

The answer doesn’t fully explain how Joule or AI Core subscribe to Event Mesh topics, classify millions of meter events, and then trigger SAP Build Process Automation or S/4HANA actions. Without that integration detail, the solution doesn’t meet the operational needs of meter‑to‑cash and outage management in real utility landscapes.

entry.title: Re: Online Validation Service - VIES Online Validation fo...
entry.content:
Dear @azlanmn,

Yes, VIES validation is also available for SAP S/4HANA on-premise deployments. SAP provides dedicated on-premise documentation for Online Validation, where VIES is explicitly listed as a use case for validating VAT registration numbers

More details below:

SAP Help – Online Validation
SAP Help – VAT Information Exchange System (VIES)
SAP Help – Online Validation for Multiple Business Partners
SAP Help – Setting Up Direct Connection for VIES Validation

Please note that the configuration is deployment-specific. In Public Cloud the setup is done via SSCUI 105630, while in on-premise systems the customer should follow the corresponding on-premise customizing and technical connection setup

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Aditya Sai Rohith Kaki, and this is my journey into SAP
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I’m Aditya Sai Rohith Kaki, currently pursuing an MBA in Marketing and IT at ICFAI Business School, Hyderabad.

If someone had asked me a few years ago where I’d be today, I probably wouldn’t have imagined introducing myself to a global SAP community.

I started my career in software testing, working with teams at Accenture and Flecks Labs, where I learned the importance of quality, teamwork, and solving problems with data. Later, during my MBA, I had the opportunity to intern with Varun Beverages (PepsiCo). Spending time in the market, interacting with retailers, analyzing sales data, and building dashboards showed me how technology and business come together to make better decisions.

That curiosity eventually brought me to SAP, and I’m excited to keep learning from this amazing community.  

What is your professional life dream?

My dream is simple to become someone who solves real business problems.

Whether that’s as a Business Consultant, Product Manager, or in another role, I want to bridge the gap between business and technology. I enjoy understanding how businesses work and finding smarter ways to improve processes using data and digital solutions. That’s one of the reasons SAP interests me so much.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

Five years from now, I hope to be working on large-scale digital transformation projects, helping businesses make better decisions through technology.

I also want to keep learning because technology changes every day, and I believe staying curious is one of the most valuable skills anyone can have.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

One experience I’ll always remember was my internship at Varun Beverages (PepsiCo).

Instead of sitting behind a desk, I spent weeks visiting retail stores, talking to shop owners, understanding customer preferences, analyzing competitors, and later converting those insights into Power BI dashboards.

It wasn’t just about numbers it was about seeing how small decisions in the market could make a big difference for a business. That experience made me appreciate the power of data-driven decision-making.  

What do you imagine is the future of AI and Enterprise Technology?

I don’t think AI will replace people.

I think it will help people make faster and better decisions.

The future belongs to professionals who understand both business and technology. Platforms like SAP will become even more intelligent, helping companies predict trends, automate repetitive work, and focus more on innovation. I’m excited to be part of that journey.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One lesson my parents have always reminded me of is:

“Stay grounded, keep learning, and never stop putting in honest effort.”

Life doesn’t always go as planned, but consistency and patience eventually open doors. That’s something I try to carry with me every day.

And now I’d love to hear from you!

If you could learn one new skill that could shape your career over the next five years, what would it be and why?

Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments. I’d love to learn from your experiences too.

Looking forward to connecting with everyone in the SAP Community. Here’s to learning, growing, and building something meaningful together!

#HelloSAPCommunity #SAPCommunity #SAPUniversityAlliances #DigitalTransformation #SAP #AI #BusinessTechnology #LearningJourney

entry.title: Re: Title: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Automatic Payme...
entry.content:
Hi byd,

Short answer: The "payment order" behavior is controlled by the Payment Order Only indicator on the payment method at country/region level (SSCUI 101972). Remove it (or use a payment method that does not have it) so the run posts the payment document and clears the supplier item directly against the bank clearing/sub-account. Everything below is Public Cloud only.

1. Exactly which setting needs to change, and in which SSCUI?

SSCUI 101972 - Set Up Payment Methods for Each Country/Region for Payment Transactions. The trigger is the Payment Order Only indicator (classic field XNOPO) in the payment method's Posting Details. When it is set, the payment run creates a payment order and posts nothing, so the supplier item stays open and is locked against manual clearing — exactly the F1612 behavior you see. Remove the indicator to switch to direct posting.

Reference (concept): SAP Help – Payment Order. Error you are hitting is expected for payment-order items: KBA 3503165.

2. What follow-on settings must be maintained (e.g. document type for payment)?

As soon as Payment Order Only is off, the posting fields become relevant again and must be filled in the same activity (SSCUI 101972), Posting Details: the Document Type for Payment (and the clearing document type). With the indicator set these fields are greyed out, which is why they may currently be empty.

In the standard (no payment order) line: the run posts Supplier → Bank Clearing / bank sub-account and clears the supplier open item immediately; the sub-account is cleared later by the bank statement. This is the normal Public Cloud outgoing-payment flow. Reference: SAP Community (by SAP) – Automatic Supplier Payment Clearing in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

3. Impact on Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) clearing?

Yes — matching logic changes, and this is actually a simplification.

With payment order: EBS clears by matching the bank line to the payment order number (classic interpretation algorithm 029), and only then is the supplier item cleared.

With direct posting: the supplier item is already cleared at runtime, so EBS no longer needs the payment-order number. The statement only has to clear the bank clearing / sub-account against the main bank G/L, using your normal EBS posting rules/search string mapping. Make sure your processing rules resolve the outgoing payment to the sub-account - maintained in Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements app.

4. How to handle open items already blocked by an existing payment order?

You need to release them by removing the existing payment order first. The change to the payment method is not retroactive; items already tied to a payment order stay locked until that order is removed.

In Manage Automatic Payments (F0770), open the run that created the payment order and delete the payment order / payment media; the supplier items are then released and become selectable again for clearing (or clear them via the next run once the method is corrected). If payment media were already generated, deletion is blocked and must be handled first in the Manage Payment Media app. References: KBA 3168196, KBA 3374400.

Note to verify:

  • Country legal requirement: Payment Order Only is a legal requirement in some countries (items must stay open until the bank confirms). Confirm switching to direct posting is acceptable for that company code's country before changing it.

Summary: Turn off Payment Order Only in the country/region payment method (SSCUI 101972) and maintain the Document Type for Payment in its Posting Details, or assign a payment method without that indicator; then remove the existing payment order in F0770 to release the currently locked items. EBS then simply clears the bank sub-account instead of matching on the payment-order number. Verify the flag is editable for your (possibly SAP-delivered) payment method and that direct posting is legally acceptable for that country. I highly recommend you also self-study the documentation on SAP Help.

Hope it helps.

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Where Did My Backups Go? Understanding Data Retention and .bacpac Files in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

If you work with SAP Commerce Cloud, you know that keeping infrastructure running at peak performance is a top priority. To maintain this efficiency and control infrastructure growth, SAP enforces a strict data retention strategy.

Today, let’s clarify exactly how the Cloud Portal treats different data retention types, from automatic backups to downloaded .bacpac files.

Understanding Your Backup and Export Options

Since SAP Commerce Cloud runs on Microsoft Azure infrastructure, managing database storage efficiently is critical. There are three distinct ways data is handled, and understanding the differences prevents unexpected data loss:

  • Standard Data Backups: These are the automated, full system backups managed by SAP. They are part of the core infrastructure and never expire.

  • Quick Data Backups: This is a self-service, point-in-time database restore mechanism available in the Cloud Portal. Note that this is purely for the database (it does not back up files). These temporary restores expire automatically after 35 days or immediately after a restore is performed.

  • Database Exports (.bacpac files): A .bacpac file is a fully decoupled logical copy containing your database schema and table data. This is not a standard self-service product functionality and is not listed in standard public documentation. To generate one, you must submit a Service Request for a "Database Export".

Why .bacpac Files Disappear Quickly

Once created via a Service Request, the .bacpac export is sent directly to your environment's hotfolder storage account so developers can download it locally. Because uncompressed database exports cause massive, unnecessary storage growth, SAP applies strict automated lifecycle rules to these temporary files.

Just like the Commerce Logs Blob Storage has a rigid, unchangeable 30 to 90-day retention policy, the hotfolder storage account enforces a 7-day retention policy for .bacpac files. After 7 days, the automated lifecycle system permanently purges them. If your team does not download the file within this window, you will need to submit a brand-new Service Request to export the database again.

How to Get the Files: Azure Storage Explorer & AzCopy

If you want an easier or faster way to grab your database exports and logs before they are permanently purged, you don't have to rely solely on manual portal downloads. You can use official Microsoft tools to connect directly to your storage accounts:

  • Microsoft Azure Storage Explorer: A user-friendly graphical interface (GUI) that lets you easily connect to your SAP Commerce Cloud Azure Blob storage, browse your containers, and download files with a few clicks.
  • AzCopy: A high-performance command-line utility designed for copying data to and from Azure Blob storage. It is perfect for moving large .bacpac files quickly and can easily be added to your automation scripts.

Important: If your team is planning a database extraction for long-term historical records, audit tracking, or intensive local offline development, make sure to capture and download the export file before the 7-day lifecycle window closes.

💡What is your strategy? Do you manually download your .bacpac files on time, or have you already automated this process?
Let’s swap ideas in the comments!

👉 Links for reference:

  • Data Backup and Restore 
  • Commerce Logs Blob Storage
  • Connecting to Microsoft Azure Blob Storage

The approver should be able to display the data for the document that requires approval in My inbox app. If you have any issues with data not being displayed in My inbox app, KBA 3203749 should be helpful.

Best regards.

entry.title: 11. SAP SuccessFactors HCM Implementation - Rehire Process Design for Historical Termination Records
entry.content:
This is part of an ongoing blog series on SAP SuccessFactors' HCM implementation. In this post, we explore how to design the Rehire Process for Historical Termination Records that are not part of EC. In the previous blog, Part 1, I covered rehire options in EC and ONB, requirements validation, dependencies, and rehire check options for existing terminated or inactive employees in Employee Central (EC) and Onboarding. The complex problem every SAP SuccessFactors HCM customer faces is how to detect rehires who are not in Employee Central.

 

This blog is part 2 – it discusses design options and recommendations for detecting historical rehires who are not in Employee Central.

 

Customer Requirement Scenario:

 

A customer has been running a legacy HR system for more than 25 years and is now implementing SAP SuccessFactors HCM. As part of the data migration strategy, only the top-of-stack employee data is being migrated to Employee Central (EC), which aligns with SAP's leading practice recommendation. As a result, approximately 25 years of terminated employee records will remain in the legacy system and will not be available in EC after go-live.

The customer requested a detailed solution design to identify, and process rehires for these historical terminated employees after the SAP SuccessFactors EC goes live.

As discussed in my previous blog, when a terminated employee record exists in Employee Central, the standard rehire checks in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding (ONB) and Employee Central automatically detect a potential match and provide HR administrators with the option to Rehire on Old Employment or Rehire with New Employment, depending on the business scenario.

However, if the terminated employee record is not available in Employee Central, the system treats the individual as a new hire by default, resulting in the creation of new Person IDs, User IDs, Employment IDs, and Employment records. For organizations that want to preserve employee history, maintain compliance, or recognize prior service, this behavior may not meet their business requirements.

Based on my implementation experience, I have successfully designed and implemented three approaches to address this requirement. Among these, the first option is my preferred and most strongly recommended approach because it is simple, scalable, cost-effective, and aligns well with SAP SuccessFactors architecture.

 

Solution Design  1) Migrate historical terminated records with a minimum set of data/portlets:

The cleanest approach is migrating all termination records with a minimum set of data. As part of the migration, we identify terminated records and load them with a minimal data set after discussing with the business which attributes are critical; the remaining mandatory attributes will use default values. While this adds additional effort to the data migration scope, it makes the Rehire process very clean.

This is the recommended approach, in my view. If a customer has thousands of terminated records and migrating them as part of the Employee Central (EC) implementation requires significant effort, they could migrate them after EC is live. However, I strongly recommend doing it as part of the initial EC migration itself.

 

Since we are only going to load a few templates (Biographical, Personal, Address, Email, National ID, Employment, and Job Info) with a minimal set of real values and default values.

 

If you are migrating data from S/4HANA or SAP ERP HCM via Info Porter, you can set up a separate mapping template for terminated employees and load those records accordingly.

If you are migrating data manually using import templates or other migration tools, make sure you have the right logic in place to generate the correct data.

Data Migration Process for Terminated Records

Points to Remember: 

  • This solution adds extra effort to data migration, and that needs to be considered.
  • Bring only the top-of-stack termination record and add the hire record.
  • Try to populate only the minimum set of required fields in Job Info, use default values for foundation object attributes (Position, Department, Division, Pay Grade, Cost Center..etc).
  • If migrating all historical terminations is not an option, consider how far back you go for rehires. Based on that, migrate historical termination records.

 

Solution Design 2) Create an MDF object and load historical termination records:

This is another approach I have used in the past: when migrating historical termination records is not an option due to a high volume of records, we create a custom MDF object to maintain a minimum set of data. This includes attributes such as First Name, Last Name, Date of Birth, National ID, Termination Date, Email, and other minimum required fields.

 

  1. Migrate historical former employee data into a Historical Former Employees MDF object.
  2. If an external recruiting system is used, it can call the Historical Former Employees MDF Object OData API to identify potential rehires during the recruiting process.
  3. In SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting (RCM), a rehire check can be implemented using the MDF object to detect potential rehires before the hiring process progresses.
  4. Once onboarding is initiated, build a process to validate the employee against the Historical Former Employees MDF object, flag potential rehires, and trigger the appropriate action. This can be implemented using a business rule.
  5. For employees hired directly in Employee Central (EC), a business rule can be configured during the hire process to check the Historical Former Employees MDF object, flag potential rehires, and enable the HR administrator to take the appropriate rehire action.

 

Solution Design 3)Build a process to look up the external system where historical HR data resides:

 

This is the third option we have used to perform a rehire check. Assume the customer is moving from S4HANA to SAP SuccessFactors HCM, will use S4HANA for payroll, and will keep complete historical HR Data.

 

The above options are not feasible for various reasons, i have used this design to perform a lookup in the S4HANA system to check for rehire. If any matches are found, it returns the details with the Employee ID and other attributes. Based on this, you take action.

 

 

  • Create an OData or SOAP service in SAP S/4HANA that returns historical employee data based on input parameters. Expose the service through SAP API Management so that it can be securely consumed by external systems.
  • If an external recruiting system is used, it can call the SAP S/4HANA OData service to identify potential rehires during the recruiting process.
  • In SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting (RCM) or SmartRecruiters, a rehire Check can be implemented by calling the SAP S/4HANA OData service through SAP Integration Suite, triggered by Intelligent Services events.
  • Once onboarding is initiated, SAP Integration Suite can invoke the SAP S/4HANA OData service to validate whether the individual is a potential rehire, flag the record, and trigger the appropriate rehire action ( Rehire with old employment /New Employment).

entry.title: 2026 SAP Cloud ALM Partner Readiness Series
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP Community blog for the 2026 SAP Cloud ALM Partner Readiness Series. We'll use this blog for content updates, links to decks and other materials, and to ask questions of our experts. 

  • Orientation sessions on July 14 or July 16. 
  • Complete the SAP Cloud ALM Beginner Learning Journey to prepare for the series. 
  • Series starts on August 4th. 
  • One session per week, 60 minutes per session. 
  • Opportunity to earn a Record of Achievement at the end of the series by passing an assessment quiz. 
  • Add comments / questions below for our experts. 
  • Register Here! 

 

Overview

SAP Cloud ALM is essential for any cloud transformation journey with SAP. It is not a question of if all customers will run an SAP Cloud ALM tenant in the future, but when. This series will help you get transparency about all SAP Cloud ALM use cases for you as a SAP Partner. 

  • Learn how SAP Cloud ALM can help to deliver your SAP Cloud projects, such as SAP Cloud ERP public or private, SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Ariba, etc. more efficiently. SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate SAP cloud and hybrid business solutions for your customers. You benefit from an out-of-the-box, native cloud solution, designed as the central entry point to manage customers’ SAP landscape with content-driven guided implementation and highly automated operations.
  • This series will give you the chance to get the required information about SAP Cloud ALM, to be prepared to use the tool and integrate it into your delivery strategy.
  • SAP Cloud ALM is the central element of the agent-led toolchain to integrate with many SAP tools in the overall RISE with SAP Methodology and it’s the main platform which supports the execution of any cloud transformation with many AI use cases included. 
  • There will be at least 10 hours of content, demos,  and discussions with SAP experts about current features and future roadmap. As a partner, you should use the opportunity to check against your project situation how you could benefit from SAP Cloud ALM usage. Sessions will be one hour, one per week. Join the orientation session on July 14 or July 16 to learn more and submit your questions about the series.
  • Enhance your value and participate in an SAP Transformation consultant assessment which is included as last session of the series. Passing the assessment will lead to a Record of Achievement being issued to the individual.
  • This training is also a very good opportunity to get ready for the exam leading to become SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM.

Learning objectives

  • Understand the SAP Cloud ALM based Transformation Management approach of SAP in the context of RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP projects
  • In-depth Knowledge: Gain comprehensive insights into SAP Cloud ALM, including its implementation and operations features
  • Learn about latest innovations in SAP Cloud ALM regarding AI assisted migration
  • Expert Guidance: Learn from SAP Cloud ALM experts and get access to partner best practices and enabling guidance
  • Assessment to achieve the SAP Transformation consultant badge and rehearsal for the certification
  • Basics for the SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM exam. (certification exam - not part of the camp!)

The obstacle we face is that SAP already has the revenue (in the form of pre-purchased exam attempts).  They also aren't going to lose customers, nor customers representing a significant portion of their revenue (small fish).  Anyone having paid for an exam is likely already heavily invested in SAP, and only impacting individuals, not overall organizational goals.  Likely the case of a hot potato within SAP - good intentions on modifying exam format, bad execution, that clearly wasn't tested to the extent required

entry.title: Re: To add the image in UI builder need to put the url wh...
entry.content:
Hello @Ritumbhara,

The image URL configured in UI Builder must point to a location that is accessible to everyone who needs to load the screen. From the URL you shared, it looks like the image is stored in a personal SharePoint/OneDrive location (people.ey.com), so other users likely don't have permission to access it.
As far as I know, SAP Customer Data Cloud doesn't provide built-in storage for media assets (such as images) that can be referenced from UI Builder. The Image field simply expects a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.
A common approach is to host images on a CDN, a public web server, or another static hosting solution (for example, AWS S3 + CloudFront, Azure Blob Storage, or your company's web server) and reference those URLs from UI Builder.

Regards,
Oleh.

entry.title: Re: Font customizations in CDC
entry.content:
Hello @Ritumbhara,

Yes, it is possible to use custom fonts in SAP CDC.
The fonts are typically loaded using the standard CSS @font-face rule and then applied to the required UI Builder elements.
For example:

@font-face {
    font-family: 'Museo Sans';
    src: url('https://your-domain.com/fonts/MuseoSans-Regular.woff2') format('woff2');
    font-weight: 400;
    font-style: normal;
    font-display: swap;
}

.gigya-screen-caption {
    font-family: 'Museo Sans', sans-serif;
}
 
However, the error you're seeing is not related to the CSS syntax itself. The browser is reporting a CORS error:
No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is present...
 
This usually means that the server hosting the font files is not allowing cross-origin requests. Font files (.woff, .woff2, .otf, etc.) are subject to CORS, so the hosting server must return the appropriate Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.

If your fonts are hosted under something like:

https://domain/assets/fonts/...
then that server needs to be configured to allow the fonts to be requested from the origin where your CDC screens are running.

So:

  • Define the fonts using @font-face in your Custom CSS.
  • Apply the font family to the required CDC UI elements.
  • Host the font files on a server that allows cross-origin requests (CORS).

If the CORS headers cannot be configured on the server hosting the fonts, you'll need to host the font files on another server or CDN where you have control over the HTTP response headers.

Regards,
Oleh.

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud] How to Transfer a Customer Line Item with Negative Balance to a Vendor Line Item
entry.content:
Hello,

I would appreciate your advice on the following matter.

【System Environment】
SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

【What I'm trying to achieve】
I would like to create payment data after recognizing a customer line item
with a negative balance as a payable (vendor-side liability).
As a means to achieve this, within the same Business Partner (BP), I am
planning to transfer a customer line item with a negative balance to a
vendor line item.

Rather than clearing the customer line item against the vendor line item,
I would like the following:
- Customer line item: cleared
- Vendor line item: a new open item (payable) is created

【Desired process flow】
1. A receivable (accounts receivable) is created for the customer
2. A return occurs, causing the customer balance to become negative
(credit balance)
3. The customer line item with the negative balance is transferred to a
vendor line item (payable) under the same BP code
4. Payment data is created based on the vendor line item (assuming the
Automatic Payment Program / F110)
5. A payment advice is issued

【Question】
Could you advise which app/transaction can achieve step 3 above
("transfer from customer line item to vendor line item")?
① I am considering the "Clear Incoming Payments" app (App ID: F0773) —
is this achievable with that app?
② Are there any other apps or configurations required besides the above?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Application could not be started due to technical iss...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please take a look into below possible resolution:

Possible Resolution Steps

Step 1: Disable "Asynchronous Module Loading" in Work Zone Site Settings

This is the most commonly confirmed fix for Shell module resolution errors in SAP Build Work Zone. [https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/workzone-bpa-task-error-quot-failed-to-resolve-dependencies-of/qaq-p/13893628]

Open your SAP Build Work Zone site manager.

Navigate to Site Settings of the affected Work Zone site.

Locate the "Asynchronous Module Loading" flag. Disable this setting. Save and republish the site, then retest the navigation scenario.

SAP Note 0003527309 documents this fix for similar Shell dependency resolution failures in Work Zone.

Step 2: Check for a Known SAP Note / Patch for "h.loaded"

A similar issue where a CAP/Fiori UI stopped loading from Work Zone (with ShellServices errors) was resolved by applying SAP Note 3525583. [https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cap-ui-not-loading-when-opening-from-sap-build-workzone/qaq-p/13890385]

Log in to SAP for Me (me.sap.com). Search for SAP Note 3525583 and check if it applies to your system version. Also search for notes related to keywords: h.loaded, SAP Build Work Zone, RAP Fiori Elements, Shell plugin. Apply any relevant correction notes or patches to your system.

entry.title: Extending SAP Joule with SharePoint Grounding and MCP
entry.content:
What You Will Learn

In this blog post you will learn how to:

  • Ground SAP Joule in SharePoint documents using SAP AI Core Document Grounding so that Joule can answer questions directly from your document library
  • Build a chunking and embedding pipeline in SAP AI Launchpad that keeps the vector index fresh as documents change
  • Create custom Joule skills and agents in SAP Build that chain document search, structured extraction, and S/4HANA API calls into a fully automated workflow — no copy-paste, no form-filling
  • Connect a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server as a Joule toolkit so that warehouse operations can evolve independently of the agent definition

By the end of this two-part series, a warehouse operator can type "Process delivery note 54321" in Joule and have an inbound delivery created in S/4HANA automatically.

Prerequisites

Before you start, make sure you have:

  • An SAP BTP subaccount with entitlements for SAP AI Core (Extended plan) and SAP Build Process Automation
  • An SAP AI Launchpad instance connected to your AI Core service
  • An SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant with the Inbound Delivery A2X API enabled, or access to SAP API Business Hub sandbox
  • A Microsoft 365 tenant with a SharePoint site containing your delivery note documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, or PPTX)
  • Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator access in Microsoft Entra ID — or a contact who has it — to grant admin consent for API permissions
  • Basic familiarity with SAP Build (Joule Studio) and BTP destinations

Introduction

SAP Joule is SAP's generative AI copilot embedded across the SAP ecosystem. It understands SAP transactional data well out of the box — but enterprise knowledge often lives outside SAP. Delivery notes, vendor contracts, material specs, and SOPs typically sit in SharePoint, Teams, or network drives. Joule cannot act on what it cannot read.

This walkthrough shows how we connected those two worlds. The result is a Joule Capability Package called Custom Agent Ideation that imports three reusable building-block packages and adds two orchestration agents and four Joule skills on top:

  • searchDeliveryNotes — instant vector search over SharePoint PDFs directly from Joule
  • deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery — end-to-end automation: search → extract → map → create inbound delivery in S/4HANA
  • createInboundDelivery — direct inbound delivery creation when you already have the data
  • warehouseTaskCreator — MCP-powered agent that creates warehouse tasks from an inbound delivery number

Architecture Overview

Joule Extensibility — Reference Architecture

The SAP reference architecture for Integrating and Extending Joule defines the canonical pattern that underpins everything in this walkthrough. At its core, Joule sits in the middle — connected to SAP BTP for extensibility, SAP Cloud Identity Services for authentication, and the broader SAP application estate (S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, Ariba, Concur) through a unified integration layer.

extend joule with joule studio

Source: SAP Architecture Center — Integrating and Extending Joule (Ref-Arch 464deb)

Joule's extensibility operates across four capability patterns:

Pattern Description

TransactionalNatural language triggers direct backend processes — purchase orders, approvals, document creation
NavigationalGuides users to the right application or screen without manual navigation
InformationalDelivers knowledge from SAP Help, customer documents, or indexed content via RAG
AnalyticalSurfaces business insights and performance metrics on demand

This walkthrough implements the Informational pattern (SharePoint document grounding) and the Transactional pattern (automated inbound delivery creation and warehouse task management) — showing how both can work together in a single Joule Capability Package built with Joule Studio in SAP Build.

This Demo — Solution Architecture

The diagram below shows how the components in this walkthrough connect at runtime. The Joule Capability Package (Custom Agent Ideation) sits on top of three reusable building-block packages and exposes four skills that Joule routes to based on the user's intent.

Imported Package Version Purpose

SharePoint Document Grounding Search1.1.0AI Core vector search over SharePoint
Delivery Note Assistant1.1.0SBPA action to retrieve and extract delivery note details
Inbound Delivery (A2X)1.1.3SBPA action to create inbound deliveries in S/4HANA

These three packages are SAP-maintained building blocks. You write only the orchestration logic that is unique to your scenario.

Custom Agent Ideation project overview showing imported package dependencies

Part 1: SharePoint Integration

Step 1 — Set Up SharePoint Grounding

This step produces the four credentials that AI Core needs to pull documents from SharePoint. You will set up a SharePoint site, register an Azure app, grant the right permissions, and validate access before wiring anything into AI Core.

1a — Prepare the SharePoint Site

  1. Sign in to sharepoint.com and create a new Communication site (recommended for document repositories).
  2. Upload your delivery note files. Supported formats include PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and images.
  3. Note the site URL: https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name> — you will need it in Step 2.

1b — Register an Azure App in Microsoft Entra ID

  1. Go to Azure Portal → Entra ID → App registrations → New registration.
  2. Name the app (e.g., SAP-DocumentGrounding) and complete the registration.
  3. From the Overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID.
  4. Go to Certificates & Secrets → Client Secrets → New client secret.
    • Copy the Value immediately — it is shown only once. The Secret ID (GUID) is not the secret value.
  5. Go to API Permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Application permissions.
    • Add Sites.Selected (Application type — "Access selected site collections").
    • Do not use Sites.Read.All — Sites.Selected is the least-privilege option and the correct permission for Document Grounding.
    • Click Grant admin consent. The Sites.Selected permission must show a ✅ status before you continue.
Caution: Admin consent requires a Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator. If you do not have that role, you cannot approve this yourself.
  • SAP employees: raise a request with your SharePoint / Azure Entra ID admin team to grant consent on the app registration.
  • External partners / customers: work with your organisation's SharePoint administrator to click Grant admin consent on your behalf.
Without this step, all API calls will return 403 Forbidden even if the permissions appear in the list.

Azure App Registration overview page showing Application and Directory (tenant) IDsAzure API permissions showing Sites.Selected granted with admin consent

1c — Grant the App Access to the SharePoint Site

Admin consent in Entra ID grants API-level access, but you also need to grant site-level read permission using PnP PowerShell:

Install-Module PnP.PowerShell

Connect-PnPOnline -Url "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name>" -Interactive

Grant-PnPAzureADAppSitePermission `
  -AppId "<CLIENT_ID>" `
  -DisplayName "SAP Document Grounding" `
  -Site "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name>" `
  -Permissions Read

1d — Validate Access (Optional but Recommended)

Get an access token and confirm the Graph API can reach your site before wiring it into AI Core:

# Get access token
curl -X POST "https://login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=<CLIENT_ID>&client_secret=<CLIENT_SECRET>&scope=https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"

# Validate site access
curl -X GET "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/sites/<SHAREPOINT_DOMAIN>:/sites/<SITE_NAME>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>"

A 200 response with site metadata confirms everything is wired correctly.

At the end of this step you should have four values ready:

Value Where to find it

Tenant IDEntra ID → App registration → Overview
Client IDEntra ID → App registration → Overview
Client SecretEntra ID → Certificates & Secrets → Value column
SharePoint Site URLSharePoint Admin Center or browser address bar

These four values feed directly into the AI Core secret in the next step.

Part 2: AI Core Service Integration

Step 2 — Create the SharePoint Grounding Generic Secret in AI Launchpad

AI Core needs your SharePoint credentials to pull documents during indexing. Store them as a Generic Secret in SAP AI Launchpad so they are never hardcoded in pipeline configuration.

  1. Open SAP AI Launchpad → AI Core Administration → Generic Secrets.
  2. Click Create and name the secret, e.g., sharepoint-grounding-secret.
  3. Paste in the credentials JSON:
{
  "tenantId": "<AZURE_TENANT_ID>",
  "clientId": "<CLIENT_ID>",
  "clientSecret": "<CLIENT_SECRET>",
  "sharepointSiteUrl": "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site>"
}

AI Launchpad Generic Secret form showing Application (Client) ID fieldAI Launchpad Generic Secret form showing Client Secret field

  1. Save. This secret will be referenced by the chunking pipeline in Step 3.

Step 3 — Create the Document Chunking Pipeline

The chunking pipeline reads documents from SharePoint, splits them into semantically meaningful chunks, embeds them, and writes them to a vector collection in AI Core.

  1. In AI Launchpad, navigate to Pipelines → Create Pipeline.
  2. Select the Document Grounding template.
  3. Set the source to SharePoint and reference the secret from Step 2.
  4. Set chunk size and overlap. Recommended starting values: 512 tokens, 50-token overlap.
  5. Name the target collection — e.g., delivery-notes.
  6. Run the pipeline and wait for completion. Note the final document and chunk counts.
Tip: If you update documents in SharePoint regularly, schedule the pipeline to run on a recurring cadence so the vector index stays current.

Step 4 — Verify the AI Core Search Endpoint

Once the collection is populated, verify the search endpoint is working. This is what the searchDeliveryNotes skill will call at runtime.

  1. In AI Launchpad, go to Grounding → Collections and select delivery-notes.
  2. Note the Collection ID — you will use this as the id in the grounding filter in Part 2, Step 5.
  3. Set the retrieval config: Top K = 5, Similarity threshold = 0.7.
  4. Run a test query — e.g., "delivery note vendor material PO number" — and confirm chunks return correctly.

The SharePoint Document Grounding Search package imported in the project wraps this endpoint as a callable Joule function.

 

Part 3: SAP Build

Step 5 — Build the Actions

Actions in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) wrap external API calls as reusable steps. The project imports two action packages.

5a — SharePoint Delivery Note Search (from Delivery Note Assistant package)

This action calls the AI Core Orchestration endpoint and returns the delivery note content as structured JSON. Under the hood it combines document grounding with an LLM extraction step in a single request.

  • Action identifier: post_getDeliveryNoteDetails
  • Destination: mocked_JOULEDELIVERYNOTEDEST (points to your AI Core endpoint)
  • Input: query (string) — the search phrase passed by the agent
  • Output: deliverycontentdetails — the extracted delivery note fields as a JSON string

The SBPA function is called asynchronously via the Joule skill integration gateway and uses long-polling (14 s timeout) to wait for the AI Core response.

The full request body sent to AI Core:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "gpt-4o",
        "model_params": { "max_tokens": 1000, "temperature": 0 }
      },
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "input_params": ["query"],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput",
          "filters": [
            { "id": "<YOUR_COLLECTION_ID>", "data_repository_type": "vector" }
          ]
        }
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "system",
            "content": "You are a delivery note extraction assistant. Extract all fields from the document context and return ONLY a valid JSON object with no markdown, no extra text, no code fences."
          },
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "Document context:\n{{?groundingOutput}}\n\nExtract all delivery note details and return as JSON."
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": { "query": "<natural language search query>" }
}

Key points to note:

  • filters.id — replace <YOUR_COLLECTION_ID> with the Collection ID from Step 4.
  • data_repository_type: "vector" — must be snake_case. Using dataRepositoryType (camelCase) returns a 400 error.
  • {{?groundingOutput}} — the AI Core Orchestration service injects retrieved document chunks here before calling the LLM.
  • The system prompt forces a clean JSON response with no markdown fences, because the downstream agent parses the content string directly.

5b — Create Inbound Delivery (from Inbound Delivery (A2X) package)

This action calls the S/4HANA A2X Inbound Delivery API to create the delivery record.

  • Action identifier: post_A_InbDeliveryHeader
  • Destination: mocked_inbound_delivery (points to your S/4HANA or API Hub endpoint)

Field Source in agent S/4HANA field

supplierdeliverydocumentDeliveryNoteNoDeliveryDocumentBySupplier
deliverydateDeliveryDate (ISO format)DeliveryDate
actualdeliveryquantityDeliveryQtyActualDeliveryQuantity
purchaseorderPONumberReferenceSDDocument
purchaseorderitemLineNoReferenceSDDocumentItem
batchnumberBatchNoBatch

Step 6 — Create the AI Agent Project in SAP Build

The project in SAP Build groups everything together. Create a new AI Agent Project and import the three dependency packages.

  1. In SAP Build, go to Lobby → Create → Build an Automated Process → AI Agent.
  2. Name it Custom Agent Ideation, set description to Customized Joule Agent for Public Cloud ERP.
  3. Import the three dependency packages:
    • SharePoint Document Grounding Search
    • Delivery Note Assistant
    • Inbound Delivery (A2X)
  4. Configure the system aliases under Settings:
    • SbpaService → destination: SAP_SBPA_API
    • APIAGENTCALLBACK → destination: AC_callback_api

These aliases let the function configuration in SAP Build refer to the SBPA integration gateway without hardcoding URLs.

Step 7 — Build the Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent

This is the orchestration brain of the solution. The agent runs the full Search → Extract → Map → Create → Confirm flow automatically, without any user intervention.

Agent configuration:

agent: Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent
base_model: gpt-5-minimal
iterations: 25
default_output_format: markdown

The automated process the agent follows:

  1. On any delivery note query, immediately call Search Delivery Notes with the query: "delivery note number, vendor, material, quantity, PO number, line item"
  2. Parse the JSON response and extract: DeliveryNoteNo, DeliveryDate, PONumber, LineNo, MaterialNo, DeliveryQty, BatchNo, Warehouse
  3. Map the extracted values to the inbound delivery API fields (see table in Step 5b)
  4. Call Create Inbound Delivery with the mapped parameters
  5. Reply to the user with a confirmation including the delivery note number, PO, quantity, and date

The agent instruction explicitly states: "The agent must never ask the user for any of these values or present options; the process must be strictly automated and sequential."

Content safety filtering is applied to all inputs (Azure Content Safety, threshold 4 for hate/self-harm/sexual/violence).

Expertise:

You are an expert in automating logistics workflows, specializing in the seamless extraction, mapping, and orchestration of delivery note data to inbound delivery processes without user intervention.

Instructions:

**Purpose & Objectives**
The Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent assists users in fully automating the creation of inbound deliveries from delivery notes. Its objectives are:
- Automatically search for and extract delivery note information upon receiving relevant queries.
- Parse, map, and transfer all required delivery fields for inbound delivery creation, without requesting further input from the user.
- Ensure successful creation is promptly confirmed to the user.

**Process**
1. When the user requests a delivery note-related task (such as providing a delivery note number, vendor, material, PO, or line item):
   - Immediately call the 'Search Delivery Notes' tool with the query: "delivery note number, vendor, material, quantity, PO number, line item".
2. Upon receiving the JSON output from the tool:
   - Parse the JSON string and extract these fields:
     - DeliveryNoteNo
     - DeliveryDate
     - PONumber
     - LineNo
     - MaterialNo
     - DeliveryQty
     - BatchNo
     - Warehouse
   - Convert *DeliveryDate* with same format of '2026-04-27T00:00:00'
   - Map the extracted values as follows for the next step:
     - SupplierDeliveryDocument = DeliveryNoteNo
     - DeliveryDate = DeliveryDate
     - ActualDeliveryQuantity = DeliveryQty
     - PurchaseOrder = PONumber
     - PurchaseOrderItem = LineNo
     - BatchNumber = BatchNo
3. Using the mapped values, automatically invoke the 'Create Inbound Delivery' tool with the mapped input parameters.
4. Upon successful execution, confirm to the user what was created (including delivery note and PO details).
- The agent must never ask the user for any of these values or present options; the process must be strictly automated and sequential as above.

**Tooling**
- 'Search Delivery Notes': Retrieves delivery note and related document data based on query parameters.
- 'Create Inbound Delivery': Consumes extracted and mapped values to generate inbound deliveries.
- JSON Parsing: The agent must be capable of handling and extracting the required fields from complex JSON responses and mapping them precisely as outlined.
- The orchestration logic must guarantee a hands-free, predictable workflow for the user.

Additional Context:

**Communication**
- Maintain a professional, concise, and transactional tone; avoid any unnecessary small talk or ambiguity.
- Always state exactly what was done and which identifiers were involved.
- Never prompt the user for information or present choices about delivery note or inbound delivery details.

**Guidelines**
- If any required value is missing in the JSON output, clearly state which information was unavailable, but do not request user input to supply it.
- Follow the sequence of tools and logic strictly: Search → Extract → Map → Create → Confirm.
- Avoid speculation or assumptions—work strictly with the output you have.

**Examples**
**User:** "Please process this delivery note."
**Agent:** *[Searches delivery notes, extracts all required fields, creates inbound delivery, then replies:]*
"Inbound delivery created for Delivery Note #54321 (PO #98765, Qty: 100, Date: 2024-06-10)."

**User:** "Can you make an inbound delivery from this note?"
**Agent:** *[Performs end-to-end flow automatically; responds with confirmation of creation and details.]*

**Edge Case:**
**User:** "Process this delivery note."
**Agent:** *[If deliveryNoteNo is missing after parsing JSON:]*
"Unable to create inbound delivery: delivery note number was not found in the search results."

Step 8 — Create the Joule Skills (Scenarios)

Skills are what Joule sees — each scenario maps a user intent to a function, which in turn calls an agent or directly invokes an action. The project exposes four skills:

Skill (Scenario) Joule trigger description Target function

searchDeliveryNotesUser asks about delivery notes, vendors, materials, or shipmentssearchDeliveryNotes (direct action)
deliveryNotetoInboundDeliveryUser wants to convert a delivery note to an inbound deliverydeliveryNotetoInboundDelivery (agent-orchestrated)
createInboundDeliveryUser wants to create an inbound delivery directlycreateInboundDelivery (direct action)
warehouseTaskCreatorUser wants to create a warehouse taskwarehouseTaskCreator (agent-orchestrated via MCP)

Each agent-orchestrated skill works the same way at the function level: take the raw user message, pass it to the agent, and return the agent's response as markdown.

# deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery function (simplified)
- type: agent-request
  name: deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery
  body: <? agentRequestBody ?>
  result_variable: agentResponse

Step 9 — Build the Warehouse Task Creator Agent (MCP)

The Warehouse Task Creator is a second agent that handles a different part of the workflow: once an inbound delivery exists, create the corresponding warehouse task via the MCP server.

Agent configuration:

agent: Warehouse Task Creator
base_model: gpt-5-minimal

Process:

  1. Receive a user request containing an inbound delivery number
  2. Validate that the number is present and correctly formatted (numeric)
  3. Call the MCP warehouse task creation tool
  4. Return the task ID, or a clear error message if something goes wrong

MCP toolkit binding:

toolkits:
  - name: WarehouseTaskCreator
    type: mcp
    config:
      description: Create warehouse task based on inbound delivery document number.
      destination: warehouse-task-mcp
      namespace: warehouseTaskCreator.WarehouseTaskCreator0
      path: /mcp
      timeout: 60

The type: mcp is what distinguishes this agent from the previous one. Instead of calling an SBPA action group, it calls a Model Context Protocol endpoint at /mcp on the warehouse-task-mcp destination. Joule discovers available tools dynamically via the protocol — new warehouse operations can be added to the MCP server without touching the agent definition.

Expertise:

You are an expert in creating warehouse tasks in the MCP system, specializing in processing inbound delivery numbers and efficiently triggering warehouse operations based on user requests.

Instructions:

**Purpose & Objectives**
The Warehouse Task Creator assists users in automatically creating new warehouse tasks in the MCP system using the inbound delivery number provided in their prompt.
Success criteria include:
- Promptly creating a warehouse task whenever a valid inbound delivery number is given.
- Ensuring the user receives clear confirmation about task creation, including task details or error messages.

**Process**
1. Receive the user's request containing an inbound delivery number.
2. Validate that the inbound delivery number is present and correctly formatted.
3. When the number is valid, call the MCP warehouse task creation tool and submit the inbound delivery number as input.
4. Relay the response from the MCP tool back to the user, summarizing task details or clearly stating any encountered issues.
5. If input is missing or invalid, ask the user for clarification or a valid inbound delivery number.

**Tooling**
- MCP Warehouse Task Creation Tool: Use this tool to create a warehouse task by inputting the provided inbound delivery number. Always match the user's request to the required format before calling the tool.

Additional Context:

**Communication**
- Keep responses concise, straightforward, and informative.
- Avoid ambiguity—if the inbound delivery number is missing or incorrect, state the issue directly.

**Guidelines**
- Never guess or invent inbound delivery numbers; always ask the user to provide one if missing or unclear.
- Confirm task creation clearly and include key details or relevant error messages.

**Examples**
**User:** "Create a warehouse task for inbound delivery 5273849"
**Agent:** "A new warehouse task has been created for inbound delivery 5273849. Task ID: WT-00012345."
**User:** "Create a task for inbound delivery 123xyz"
**Agent:** "The inbound delivery number '123xyz' is invalid. Please provide a valid numeric inbound delivery number."

Step 10 — Configure the MCP Destination

The MCP server exposes warehouse operations as MCP-compliant tool definitions. The BTP destination is what connects the SAP Build agent to your deployed server.

  1. Deploy the MCP server to BTP (Cloud Foundry or Kyma). The tool namespace is warehouseTaskCreator.WarehouseTaskCreator0.
  2. In BTP Cockpit, create a destination named warehouse-task-mcp pointing to your deployed MCP service URL.
  3. Configure authentication (Bearer token recommended).
  4. In SAP Build, the warehouseTaskCreator agent picks up this destination automatically via the type: mcp toolkit binding.
  5. Test end-to-end by triggering the warehouseTaskCreator skill in Joule with a real inbound delivery number.

End-to-End Walkthrough

With everything deployed, here is what a warehouse operator experiences in Joule:

Scenario A — Search for a delivery note:

"Show me delivery note from Vendor ABC for PO 4500012345"

Joule routes to searchDeliveryNotes → SBPA calls AI Core Document Grounding Search → returns chunks from the SharePoint PDF → Joule displays the delivery note details.

Scenario B — Fully automated inbound delivery creation:

"Process delivery note 54321"

Joule routes to deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery → agent searches SharePoint → extracts and maps all fields automatically → calls S/4HANA A2X API → confirms:

"Inbound delivery created for Delivery Note #54321 (PO #98765, Qty: 100, Date: 2026-04-27)."

Scenario C — Warehouse task creation:

"Create a warehouse task for inbound delivery 5273849"

Joule routes to warehouseTaskCreator → agent validates the number → calls MCP server → confirms:

"A new warehouse task has been created for inbound delivery 5273849. Task ID: WT-00012345."

Summary

Step Component What Was Built

1Azure / SharePointApp Registration with Sites.Selected for least-privilege grounding access
2AI LaunchpadGeneric Secret storing SharePoint credentials
3AI Core PipelineDocument chunking and embedding pipeline → delivery-notes vector collection
4AI Core SearchVector search endpoint verified and ready
5SAP Build Actionspost_getDeliveryNoteDetails (SharePoint) + post_A_InbDeliveryHeader (S/4HANA A2X)
6SAP Build ProjectCustom Agent Ideation with three imported packages
7Joule AgentdeliveryNotetoInboundDelivery — automated Search → Extract → Map → Create
8Joule SkillsFour scenarios: search, end-to-end automation, direct create, warehouse task
9Warehouse AgentwarehouseTaskCreator — MCP-powered agent for warehouse operations
10MCPwarehouse-task-mcp destination + deployed MCP server

Key Design Decisions

Why two separate agents instead of one?

The delivery note flow is deterministic and fully automated — it always follows Search → Create with no branching. The warehouse task flow is simpler and uses a completely different backend (MCP vs. SBPA). Keeping them separate means each agent has a tight, auditable task definition and an independent iteration budget.

Why MCP for warehouse tasks?

MCP lets the warehouse operations backend evolve independently. New tools — cycle counting, pick confirmation, stock queries — can be added to the MCP server without touching the Joule agent definition. Joule discovers new tools dynamically via the protocol.

Why import packages instead of building from scratch?

The three imported packages (SharePoint Document Grounding Search, Delivery Note Assistant, Inbound Delivery (A2X)) are SAP-maintained building blocks. Using them gives you tested action definitions and upgrade paths for free. You write only the orchestration logic that is unique to your scenario.

Next Steps

  • Extend the document scope — add more SharePoint libraries (purchase orders, goods receipts) by adding source configurations to the chunking pipeline.
  • Chain the scenarios end-to-end — route deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery output directly into warehouseTaskCreator so that a single Joule prompt covers the entire inbound process.
  • Add locale formatting — the project already includes a localize function that formats dates and numbers according to the user's regional settings before displaying results.

Related Links

  • SAP Architecture Center — Integrating and Extending Joule
  • SAP AI Core Documentation — Document Grounding
  • SAP Build Process Automation — Joule Studio
  • SAP Joule Extensibility Overview
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) — Official Specification
  • Microsoft Graph API — Sites.Selected Permission
  • PnP PowerShell — Grant-PnPAzureADAppSitePermission

Have questions or feedback? Drop a comment below — happy to help. You can also find me and my teammates (liling.jia@sap.com, xiaoming.chai@sap.com) on the SAP Community profile page.

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud] How to Transfer a Customer Line Item...
entry.content:
Hello @byd ,

Yes, you can use a balancing (clearing) G/L account as an intermediary, and this is a common accounting practice. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not provide a standard function to directly transfer a customer credit balance into a vendor payable, the recommended approach is to perform two accounting entries using a clearing G/L account. In the first entry, clear the customer credit by posting it against the clearing account. In the second entry, create a vendor payable by posting from the clearing account to the vendor reconciliation account. After these two postings, the clearing account returns to a zero balance, the customer item is fully cleared, and a new vendor open item is created, which can then be paid through the standard Manage Automatic Payments (F110 equivalent) process. This approach is fully auditable, follows standard accounting principles, and keeps the AR and AP reconciliation accounts properly separated.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: ## Question 2: App or CDS view to check the change hi...
entry.content:
Hello @byd ,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, changes to asset master data such as the Cost Center are generally tracked through standard asset change documents, allowing you to see who changed the field, when it was changed, and the old and new values using the Manage Fixed Assets application or the available Display Change Documents functionality. However, Book Value is not a master data field and therefore does not have a change history; it is a calculated value that changes based on asset transactions such as acquisitions, depreciation, transfers, and retirements. For Municipality Code (Japan localization), change history depends on whether that localization field is configured as change-document enabled. In Public Cloud, SAP does not currently provide a released standard CDS view specifically for complete Asset Master change history, so the standard approach is to use the asset applications rather than direct table or CDS access.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Upload (mass create/update) capability for Fixed Assets
entry.content:
Hello @byd ,

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2602/latest):

-Master creation is fully supported through the Migrate Your Data app using the Fixed Assets migration object, and APIs are available for integration scenarios.


-Master changes are only partially supported for mass processing. Standard SAP does not provide an Excel upload for updating existing asset masters; ongoing updates should use the Manage Fixed Assets app for individual changes or released APIs for supported fields.


-Book value adjustments and impairment postings do not have a standard mass upload capability. These must be posted through the standard Asset Accounting applications, with automation only possible through released APIs where available.


-Transfers within the same company code are supported through the standard Transfer Assets application for individual transactions, but there is no standard mass upload or migration object for bulk transfers.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: DRC - alert when invoice retrieval job fails
entry.content:
Hello @marcello-mancini ,

There is no standard SAP DRC feature that automatically sends alerts when entries appear in the Resolve Errors app. The recommended approach is to monitor the scheduled KSeF retrieval job and its integration using tools such as SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Integration Suite, or standard application job monitoring. These tools can notify administrators when a job or interface fails, allowing issues to be addressed promptly. If no monitoring solution is in place, the Resolve Errors app must be checked manually as part of operational support.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Splitting Input Tax per Cost Object
entry.content:
Hello @S0027504584 ,

The tax G/L account determination is working correctly, but the proportional inheritance of Cost Center/WBS to the Import Service Tax expense line is not standard SAP functionality. Standard tax configuration determines the tax account but does not split the tax posting based on the account assignments of the underlying expense lines. If the business requires the IST expense to follow the same distribution as the original expenses, the recommended approach is to implement an FI enhancement (such as AC_DOCUMENT, ACC_DOCUMENT, or an appropriate BAdI/BTE depending on your release) to proportionally split the tax line before the accounting document is posted. This approach preserves the correct CO account assignments and ensures accurate reporting by Cost Center and WBS.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Client Authentication EKU deprecation
entry.content:
Hi @AdamCsallokozi ,

Actually we have tried out 2 options below and both working for us. 

Option 1 - use BTP destination to generate PFX(contains private key and public key), where BTP will contain the full key information
 - This option will need to use API to retrieve pfx file
 - can either use destination UI to download the public key or API to retrieve the public key

Option 2 - use OpenSSL generate private key and csr file and sign via BTP destination, where BTP do not have the private key
- this option will need to use OpenSSL to generate private key and CSR as per current blog
- can use the destination UI to generate the PEM file based on the CSR instead of using the API
- can also use the UI to retrieve the public key and generate the pfx file via OpenSSL

Just curious not sure which is the prefer option in actual implementation environment, both also meet the requirement.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP EWM Document Type Mapping Explained: ERP to EWM Integration Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

During inbound delivery integration, SAP EWM relies on document type mapping to correctly interpret and process documents received from SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA. This configuration acts as a bridge between the two systems, ensuring seamless warehouse request creation, process control, and consistent document processing. In this blog, we'll explore the complete document type mapping configuration and understand its role in EWM integration.

Step 1: Document Types Check in ERP Inbound Delivery (App ID: VL33N)

Enter Inbound Delivery Number

review Item Category "ELN" and click on header details

review delivery type "EL"

Step 2: Document Types Check in EWM Inbound Delivery (App ID: /scwm/prdi)

Enter Inbound Delivery Number and execute 

Click on form view to view header details (Document Category & Document Type)

Similarly click on form view on Items to review (Item Category & Item Type)

Step 3: Define Document Type Mapping in ERP

Here ERP vs EWM header types have been mapped

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Interfaces >> ERP Integration >> Delivery Processing >> Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

This activity maps document types between SAP ERP/SAP S/4HANA and SAP EWM warehouse request document types. It enables flexible process control by supporting one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-one document type mappings. The mapping is bidirectional, ensuring seamless document integration and processing between the enterprise management system and SAP EWM.

Here ERP vs EWM Item types have been mapped

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Interfaces >> ERP Integration >> Delivery Processing >> Map Item Types from ERP System to EWM

Step 4: Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery Process

Here the profiles against document type have been configured

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Goods Receipt Process >> Inbound Delivery >> Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery Process

This activity is used to define document types for the inbound delivery process. You can modify an existing document type, copy and adapt a standard document type, or create a new document type based on your business requirements.

ERP Ref. No is Doc No: This setting allows you to use the same document number for both the LE delivery and the corresponding warehouse request in SAP EWM. The ERP reference document number (REFDOCNO) is reused as the EWM document number (DOCNO), ensuring consistent document numbering across both systems.

Step 5: Define Item Types for Inbound Delivery Process

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Goods Receipt Process >> Inbound Delivery >> Define Item Types for Inbound Delivery Process

Step 6: Define Status Profiles

Check status profiles in inbound delivery item data

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Cross-Process Settings >> Delivery - Warehouse Request >> Status Management >> Define Status Profiles

This activity is used to define status profiles for delivery document and item types. Status profiles inherit status types from predefined system profiles, allowing you to exclude non-mandatory statuses based on business requirements. If no custom status profile is assigned, the standard system profile is applied automatically.

Select Status Profile and double click on Status Types

In similar manner, configure partner profiles

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Cross-Process Settings >> Delivery - Warehouse Request >> Partner Processing >> Define Partner Profiles

This activity is used to define partner profiles for delivery document and item types. Each partner profile is assigned to a system profile and inherits its partner role settings. You can create a custom partner profile to modify partner role assignments based on specific business requirements.

Step 7: Define Document Type Determination for Inbound Process

Path: SIMG/SPRO >> SCM Extended Warehouse Management >> Extended Warehouse Management >> Goods Receipt Process >> Inbound Delivery >> Define Document Type Determination for Inbound Delivery Process

In this IMG activity, you can specify the inbound values from which a document type can be derived. When a document is created, the system uses the these settings to automatically determine the corresponding document type.

 

Ref EWM blogs

SAP S/4HANA Embedded EWM Putaway Strategy – Step-by-Step with Real Business Scenario

SAP EWM Picking for Outbound Delivery: End-to-End Scenario Demonstration with Stock Removal Strategy

If you found this guide helpful, don't miss any other SAP MM WM blogs covering real-world automation, configurations tips, and best practices for procurement, inventory management, and warehouse management.

Click to view other SAP MM WM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Thanks,

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: Designing Product Hierarchy in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm designing Product Hierarchies in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have a question about the recommended design.

Base on my understanding:

  • A Product Hierarchy ID can be assigned to multiple Sales Organization / Distribution Channel.
  • Each Sales Organization / Distribution Channel can only have one assigned Product Hierarchy. please refer to the screenshot below

 

  • Once materials are assigned to the hierarchy, its structure (such as adding or deleting nodes) can no longer be changed.

 

Because of these limitations, I'm wondering how others handle future changes to the hierarchy structure.

Do you create a new Product Hierarchy ID when structural changes are needed? If so, is it common to treat the top-level Product Hierarchy as a version (for example, PH_V1, PH_V2, or PH_2025, PH_2026) rather than as the actual business classification?

 

Or is there another recommended design or best practice?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP GUI 10-second connect timeout (NiBufIConnect / WSAEWOULDBLOCK) — network's clean
entry.content:
is backend saturation a common culprit? Hi all, hoping to tap the collective wisdom here. I've been hitting intermittent SAP GUI connection timeouts (SAP GUI 730, direct app server connection to port 3200):

partner 'x.x.x.x:sapdp00' not reached
NiBufIConnect: connection pending after 10000ms
WSAEWOULDBLOCK: Resource temporarily unavailable

I did a full client-side network validation:

  • 10-minute psping baseline to port 3200: 0% packet loss, p50 58ms, p95 87ms
  • But p99.9 is 3063ms with a few sustained latency spikes (100-237ms clusters)
  • No sample exceeded 10s during capture

My theory: network is not innocent (tail latency exists) but alone it doesn't explain the 10s breach. Suspecting a compound event — WAN latency spike + backend saturation (work process queue, DB response, enqueue lock) — where the two combined push past the SAP GUI timeout.

Would love to hear:

  1. Is NiBufIConnect timeout on initial connect commonly a backend-side issue in your experience, or should I keep digging on the network side?
  2. What server-side signals would you check first when escalating to Basis? (I'm planning to ask for dev_disp traces, SM50/SM66 history, ST03 workload data.)
  3. Any tuning recommendations for the SAP GUI connect timeout as a temporary mitigation while investigating?
  4. Anyone here running synthetic monitoring on SAP dispatcher health? Curious how you're catching these before users report them.

Any pointers appreciated. Thanks!

we would like to inform you today about the planned deactivation of older SEPA format versions.

 

In the summer of last year, we announced in the letter "Important Changes in SEPA Payments with Commerzbank" that files in SEPA formats below Version 3.0 would no longer be accepted as of March 31, 2026.

 

To ensure the smoothest possible transition, a new timeline and a phased deactivation approach have been developed. The updated timeline takes into account the still high number of submissions in older formats.

 

Current timeline for the phased deactivation of SEPA legacy formats:

 

  • As of May 1, 2026: SCT V2.5 (Fi

entry.title: Re: Looking for ODATA API from SuccessFactors LMS

Regarding your request, this specific API is still not available for SuccessFactors LMS. 

Although the functionality you are referring to is not currently part of the SAP SuccessFactors LMS module, we encourage you to submit an enhancement request to have this functionality considered within future development cycles – you can do this on our Customer Influence portal at SAP SuccessFactors | SAP Community

For detailed steps on how to raise an enhancement request please follow the KBA 2090228

We welcome inputs from customers to improve the functionality of our products. All enhancement requests are reviewed by our Product Management team and, if successful, are developed and implemented in a future product release. Peer voting is a key part of this review process, so we encourage you to provide a clear explanation so that other customers are able to understand the functionality requested.

entry.title: Re: Two Replacement Orders Created for One CBAR Returns O...
entry.content:
Likely reasons for Duplicate Replacement Orders

Multiple refund control triggers
If the returns order has both Refund Type = Replacement Product and Refund Control = Create Replacement Order (P), the system may interpret this as two separate triggers. In some CBAR configurations, the refund type itself can initiate replacement order creation, and the refund control adds another.

Item-level vs. header-level processing
If the return reason or follow-up activity is maintained at item level, the system sometimes generates replacement orders per item line. Even if you only have one product (TG11), check whether the system split the item internally (e.g., by batch, valuation type, or logistics unit).

Configuration in Returns Management customizing
In Define Refund Control (SSCUI or IMG activity), there may be overlapping entries for refund type "Replacement Product" and refund control "P". If both are active, the system executes both.

Background job duplication
In some test tenants, the replacement order creation job can run twice if the returns order is processed in multiple steps (e.g., once at save, once at logistics follow-up).

Considering the above, you typically only need Refund Control = P to create the replacement order. If you also set Refund Type = Replacement Product, the system may double-trigger. Try using one or the other consistently.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Controlling Visibility Of Content Objects In...
entry.content:
Hello @Itumeleng_Masek,

As of now, we do not have the feature available within the product to assign content objects to users dynamically or based on certain conditions. The Assignment profile can be used to assign items/programs/curricula to users based on the user's department, etc.  However, there is no provision in the Assignment profile to assign content objects to users. It can only assign items to the users. The only possible way to achieve this would be to create 4 new items with the required content objects.

We understand its importance to you, and we want to assure you that we are constantly working to expand our offerings and meet the evolving needs of our customers. If you require this to be implemented in the future road map of application in the admin side, you could raise this in the community portal via a new thread/query for an enhancement.

Regards,

Payel

entry.title: Re: Message no. KD031 : Receiver categ. PSG is not allowe...

entry.title: Re: In the PO under the Conditions tab, the PBXX price ca...
entry.content: Why you want to change the price after GR is done? Please note that PO price changes after GR/invoice are intentionally restricted by SAP to protect financial integrity. The correct way to reflect vendor price changes after GR is through invoice adjustments (credit/debit memo), not by editing the PO directly.

entry.title: Re: BTP + Joule integration
entry.content:
I intentionally kept at high-level narrative to bring the point about visualising the target 'agent architecture' since it can't be done with Joule alone.

A CAP application (with Fiori UI) which directly integrates with S/4HANA via Event Mesh, and act as the 'filter' & 'aggregator' collating the facts about the events. Once a threshold is reached (eg: every 5 mins or on invoice generation or on 10 events within a hour), a custom agent (pro-code agent) on BTP gets notified by CAP. The agent analyses and posts the information back to the CAP application. The Fiori UI allows monitoring the events and view the results/logs. Optionally, you can build a Joule integration for your CAP application to manually trigger via human messages or query the statuses in natural language.
